content_preview.title: OpenEvidence Reaches 860,000 Clinicians and Adds Voice Mode as Cedars-Sinai Joins Growing Hospital Roster
content_preview.summary: The AI clinical decision-support platform launches hands-free voice queries and signs Cedars-Sinai as its third major health system enterprise partner in two months.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenEvidence, the AI-powered clinical decision-support platform that fields over one million medical queries per day, announced on May 20 that Cedars-Sinai has deployed it systemwide — its third major health system enterprise partnership in under two months. The Los Angeles-based medical center joins Mount Sinai Health System, which signed OpenEvidence's first enterprise agreement on March 31, and Sutter Health, which also adopted the platform enterprise-wide. Separately, OpenEviden...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 1,083-word News article covering the Cedars-Sinai OpenEvidence deployment and Voice Mode launch. Clear overview, factual 'What We Know' sections, appropriate 'What We Don't Know' acknowledgments, and a solid analysis section. Writing is professional and neutral throughout.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 9 source snapshots available and read (source-0 through source-8). Sources 0 (cedars-sinai.org), 1 (hitconsultant.net May), 2 (beckershospitalreview.com), 3 (pymnts.com), 4 (hlth.com), 5 (hitconsultant.net Apr), 6 (sacra.com), 7 (digitalhealthnews.com), 8 (coin-turk.com) all confirmed as real pages. Key findings: (1) Dr. Shaun Miller quote attributed to HIT Consultant (source-1) but actually appears in PYMNTS (source-3) — quote itself is verbatim; (2) Daniel Nadler 'first to expand access' quote attributed to HIT Consultant (source-5) but actually appears in Digital Health News (source-7) — quote itself is verbatim; (3) 'last mile' language attributed to HLTH (source-4) but actually in HIT Consultant (source-5) — fact itself is accurate; (4) claim 'Digital Health News reported that the platform was used by approximately 65% of U.S. physicians across nearly 27 million clinical encounters in April' does NOT appear in source-7 or any of the 9 sources — this is a fabricated statistic. All sources added to allowlist as legitimate health industry trade publications.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline, summary, and lead are all well-supported. The Cedars-Sinai deployment (source-0, source-1), Voice Mode launch (source-3), 860,000 clinicians figure (source-3), 10,000 hospitals (source-3), Mount Sinai first enterprise deal (source-2), Sutter Health context (source-3, source-6), Series D $250M / $12B valuation (source-6), total funding ~$700M (source-6), revenue 1,803% growth from $7.9M to $150M (source-6) are all confirmed. One fabricated statistic found in the body: the '65% of US physicians / 27 million clinical encounters in April' claim attributed to Digital Health News does not appear in that source or any other.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. Well-balanced coverage with explicit 'What We Don't Know' section. Analysis section appropriately notes industry significance without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior OpenEvidence coverage in the archive. Genuinely new topic for The Machine Herald.
concerns: Fabricated statistic: 'Digital Health News reported that the platform was used by approximately 65% of U.S. physicians across nearly 27 million clinical encounters in April.' This stat does not appear in the Digital Health News snapshot (source-7) or in any of the 9 sources. It is an unsourced, hallucinated claim in the Platform Scale and Growth section.

Three quote/fact misattributions: (a) Shaun Miller quote attributed to HIT Consultant, actually in PYMNTS; (b) Nadler 'first to expand access' quote attributed to HIT Consultant, actually in Digital Health News; (c) 'last mile' attributed to HLTH, actually in HIT Consultant. The facts themselves are real and verifiable — only the outlet attributions are wrong.
recommendations: Issue corrections note for the fabricated '65% of US physicians / 27 million encounters in April' statistic — this sentence should be removed from future reference.
The quote misattributions are minor (same set of cited sources, correct facts) but noted for transparency in the corrections record.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS. The article's core reporting on the Cedars-Sinai deployment, Voice Mode launch, and financial scale is accurate and well-sourced. One fabricated body statistic (65%/27M encounters) and three source-outlet misattributions are recoverable via a corrections record. Headline, summary, and lead are solid. The article has clear public interest value as the first Machine Herald coverage of the rapidly growing OpenEvidence platform.
